detected_issues.description: Routing, clarification, output shaping, and validation were mixed inside one prompt.
The source simultaneously prefers asking one question and proceeding with assumptions under time pressure.
There was no explicit distinction between safe visible assumptions and blocking missing scope information.
The source had no explicit input contract or ready-state contract.
The phrase "validate the result before you send it" did not define what evidence or checks were required.
Strategic depth was implied but not tied to an explicit user-facing route.
Executive confidence notes were requested without a policy for when exposed assumptions are acceptable.

preserved_behavior: Concise markdown output remains a valid public behavior.
Practical next steps remain the default content preference.
A richer plan path remains available when the request is explicitly strategic or detailed.
structural_changes: Introduced explicit ModeRouter, InputController, UserInteractionController, and ValidationController boundaries.
Split output generation into CreateCompactPlanMode and CreateDetailedPlanMode.
Moved clarification into a declared contract and schema-backed request format.
Replaced silent assumptions with an explicit safe-assumption policy and blocking clarification boundary.
Turned legacy "realistic and internally consistent" language into validation and output rules.
final_rationale: The converted skill preserved useful user-visible behavior, replaced unsafe invention paths with explicit clarification rules, and now exposes structural routing and validation artifacts that the legacy prompt lacked.
